section: Production stack: robust6 vs robust8 (L731, L972-974, L1078-1083, L2090, RQ1/RQ2)
weakness: The thesis names robust6 as THE production trust classifier everywhere (abstract, L731 'production stack uses the six free features of robust6', L951, the robust6 paragraphs L1078-1099, production-stack L2090, RQ1/RQ2 answers). As of 2026-06-05 robust8 (=robust6 + rgb_mean_conf + is_grayscale) at tau=0.20 is the recommended production model; it closes robust6's grayscale trust_rgb recall hole (svan_gray R 0.577->0.681) at a ~7-10x video-confuser FP cost. The thesis presents the grayscale hole (L972-974) as an unresolved open item when an 8-feature fix now exists and is shipped. Stale production identity + understated resolution of a self-declared carve-out.
suggestion: Either (a) add a robust8 subsection: robust8 = robust6 + {rgb_mean_conf, is_grayscale} with a per-class trust_rgb threshold closes the grayscale hole at a structural video-confuser FP cost (tau-robust), and update L972-974 + L2090 to name robust8@tau0.20 as production with robust6 as the in-domain-optimal lean baseline; OR (b) if keeping robust6 as the thesis's frozen production pick, explicitly state robust8 is post-thesis and the grayscale hole is closed in follow-up work.

section: Production-verifier identity / cascade topology (L702, L746, L1260, L2090)
weakness: The deployed verifier stage is described two incompatible ways in the production-stack paragraph (L2090) and elsewhere. (1) Per-frame mlp_v5 + mlp_v5_ir_aligned run every frame and SUPERSEDE the patch verifier (L702 'both running per-frame', L1260 'cheap enough to run per frame rather than alert-gated', L2090). (2) The cascade is 'alert_gate_only' (L746, L2090 'The cascade is alert_gate_only') and L708 design-rationale still frames the alert-gated patch verifier as a live filtering stage. A per-frame verifier and an alert_gate_only cascade are mutually exclusive topologies; L2090 asserts both in one paragraph. A reader cannot tell whether the shipped verifier fires per-frame or only at the alert gate.
suggestion: State one deployed topology: production = mlp_v5/mlp_v5_ir_aligned per-frame (classifier->verifier), with 'alert_gate_only' explicitly scoped to the SUPERSEDED patch-verifier experiments only. Remove or re-scope the L2090 'The cascade is alert_gate_only' clause so it does not read as the current production topology.

weakness: The claim 'the imgsz=640 regime is unusable for Svanstrom regardless of variant' (L824) generalises from a SINGLE measured variant (retrained_v2, R=0.072) to all three RGB variants, while the same sentence concedes the baseline and hardneg_v3more @640 runs are TBD/placeholder. retrained_v2 is the variant that ALSO collapses at 1280 (R=0.306), so it is the worst-case probe, not a representative one; the baseline (R=0.961@1280) could resolve very differently @640. The Fig 6.6 caption (L775) and Threats-to-Validity (L2019) inherit the same single-variant R=0.072 as if it characterised the regime.
suggestion: Qualify: 'retrained_v2 at imgsz=640 collapses to R=0.072; the baseline and hardneg_v3more imgsz=640 runs are pending, so the regime claim is established only for the worst-case variant.' Drop 'regardless of variant' until the baseline@640 run exists.
notes: RESOLVED/ADDRESSED 2026-06-05: Resolution-and-modality-confounds para added to Threats

weakness: L1209 asserts the airplane misses 'are therefore not abstentions but active wrong classifications, which makes them recoverable by retraining the verifier with the specific FP crops as confuser-class data.' Two unverified leaps: (1) median patch prob 0.540 is read as 'active wrong classification' rather than genuine softmax-middle uncertainty (a 4-way softmax peaking at 0.54 on the non-other class is closer to abstention-adjacent than to a confident wrong class); (2) 'recoverable by retraining' is a prediction with no retrain result behind it. The patch verifier was in fact superseded by mlp_v5 without this airplane retrain ever being run, and the OOD-airplane gap is explicitly inherited by mlp_v5 (L2041), i.e. NOT recovered.
suggestion: Soften to 'the airplane crops sit in the softmax middle band, suggesting a targeted confuser-class retrain MIGHT recover them; this is recorded as a planned step (path_forward.md 3b) and is untested.' Note that mlp_v5 inherits the same gap (L2041), so recoverability is unproven.
notes: FLAG-ONLY. No eval row records an airplane-retrained patch verifier; ledger patch-catch-below-bar gives the 0.540 median + catch rates but no retrain outcome. Recoverability claim is forward-looking, not evidence-backed.

section: 2.x Numerical Comparison table (L337 IR Svanstrom scoring label)
weakness: In Table numerical_comparison the 'Svanstrom (drone, IR)' row labels this thesis's v3b numbers (P0.950/R0.973/F1 0.961) as 'IoU@0.5', while the RGB Svanstrom row directly above is 'IoP@0.5'. The backing eval row (ir_v3b_svan640_may10) records the metric as 'Svan IoP' at imgsz=640. So the IR row's IoU@0.5 label likely contradicts its own source (should be IoP@0.5) AND no resolution is shown though it is @640 vs the RGB row's @1280. A mislabelled scoring rule inside the table whose stated contribution (RQ3) is that scoring-rule disclosure must be exact is self-undermining.
suggestion: Verify the IR Svanstrom run's matching rule against the master.csv; if IoP, change the L337 cell IoU@0.5 -> IoP@0.5 and add @640. If genuinely IoU, add a note why IR uses IoU while RGB uses IoP on the same dataset. Complements row_5 (which flags the @1280 relabel) - this is the scoring-rule axis.

section: 4.2 Trust-Aware Fusion: IR>RGB superiority claim (L728, abstract L161)
weakness: L728 concludes 'the IR detector (P=0.950,R=0.973,F1=0.961) is in fact stronger than the RGB detector at imgsz=1280 (F1=0.950) on drones' and builds the whole 'choose between two competent modalities' framing on it. But the IR F1=0.961 is at imgsz=640 (per evals.ir_v3b_svan640_may10) while the RGB F1=0.950 is at imgsz=1280 - the two detectors are compared at different resolutions and the 1.1pp IR edge is inside the resolution confound. The same +IR-stronger framing seeds the abstract and the RQ narrative. Cannot claim IR>RGB without a matched-resolution comparison.
suggestion: State the resolution asymmetry explicitly (IR@640 vs RGB@1280) and downgrade 'is in fact stronger' to 'is comparable to / on par with' until a matched-imgsz IR-vs-RGB Svanstrom comparison exists; or run IR@1280. The 'different confuser-hallucination profiles' argument (the real load-bearing point) does NOT need IR to be numerically stronger and should be foregrounded instead.
notes: RESOLVED/ADDRESSED 2026-06-05: confounds para: IR>RGB resolution confound

weakness: The headline that motivates shipping robust6 over sa32 - 'classifier-only false-alert rate 0.143 vs 0.203, about 30% fewer false alerts on unseen confusers' (L1083) and the RQ1 answer (L2066) - rests on a run the same paragraph admits has a modality split: the drone (positive) surfaces use true thermal IR while the confuser (negative) surface uses grayscale-fallback IR. robust6's discriminative power is concentrated in IR features (AUROC 0.95+), so positives and negatives are separated partly by a thermal-vs-grayscale domain shift in the IR channel, not purely by drone-vs-confuser. The caveat is stated in one trailing sentence but the 30% headline, the Table robust6_pipeline confuser-fire column, and the RQ1 claim are all read as clean. This is the central production-pick justification.
suggestion: Either re-run the confuser fire-rate with thermal-IR confusers (or grayscale-IR on both arms) to remove the split, or foreground the caveat at the headline and downgrade '30% fewer false alerts' to 'lower confuser fire under the deployment fallback-IR policy, with a thermal/grayscale modality split between positives and negatives.'
notes: RESOLVED/ADDRESSED 2026-06-05: confounds para: robust6 30% modality split

weakness: The abstract (L158) says 'the scene_aware_v3more_32feat classifier brings OOD-zoo confuser fire rate from 52.1% to 10.3%'. 10.3% is the S3 (classifier + patch verifier) number; the classifier ALONE (S2) reaches only 20.5% (Table classifiers L990; L1536 'sa32 cuts fire from 52.1% only to 20.5%'). The verb 'classifier brings ... to 10.3%' mis-attributes the patch verifier's contribution to the classifier. The contributions list (L215, '52.1% -> 10.3% on the same chain') and RQ1 (L2066, 'to 10.3% with sa32') phrase it correctly; only the abstract slips.
suggestion: Reword L158 to '...the sa32 classifier-plus-verifier chain brings OOD-zoo fire from 52.1% to 10.3% (classifier alone 20.5%)' to match L215/L990/L1536.
